pages.raw_text:
w. w. Tolman, G. c. Corbaley,
DR.. T. D. Olmsted, Jacob Schiller,
d. c. Coates B. r. Ostrander and
j. Grier Long of the charter revi-
sion committee last night address
ed the Brotherhood club of the
First Presbyterian church on the
commission plan of government,
and the efforts of the committee
to outline a city charter based on
this plan for the use of Spokane.


w. w. Tolman spoke of what the
committee had agreed upon, and
was followed by a general discus,
sion in which. the individual mem
bers of the committee gave their
personal views on different phases
of the work, so far as 1t has gone.


d. c. Coates aroused some inter-
est by charging that matters of dol
lars and cents and not the good
of the individual citizen has thus
far occupied the attention of the
committee. He seemed to think
that the new charter should con-
tain more regard for the rights of
the individual and his moral and
physical welfare, rather than be
burdened with the material things.
